static.journalingPrompt: Consider journaling the answers to these questions. What guidance in your career and life search have you prayed for this week? What guidance have you been given this week?
static.oaInstructions: *(These apply to every OA.)* The online assessment is designed to be **eventually passable by everyone**. Take the attempts **in order** — you only need to pass **one**. Attempt 1 is on your own; the final attempt allows help from a TA or the instructor. If you already solved a problem, click **reset** and do it again without looking back at your previous solution.

weeks.title: BigO, Strings, Linked Lists, Sets

class.name: Find First Palindromic String in the Array
Delete Nodes from Linked List Present in Array

outside.notes: **Find and solve at least 5 other EASY problems on leetcode.**
**Solve at least 1 medium problem on leetcode.**

outside.name: Review BigO from Cracking the Coding Interview

objectives: Remember and get serious about understanding BigO runtime and memory complexity.
Remember sets, dictionaries, lists, etc and when and why we would use them.
Pointers
Strings

weeks.title: BigO, Dictionaries, 2D Arrays, Linked Lists

outside.notes: Do easy problems if you have extra time!

objectives: Identify practices that cause inflated runtime or memory efficiency and implement solutions.
Work with 2d arrays which is very popular among interviews.
Sneakily expose you to DP with pascals triangle.

weeks.title: Super Advanced Data Structures, Sets, Dictionaries, Memoizing, Strings

class2.name: Number of Good Pairs
class2.notes: *(annoying but often comes up - learn how to do in a single pass using a dictionary)*

objectives: Implement the most complicated of data structures keeping an eye on runtime and space efficiency
Sets, dictinoaries, and strings!

weeks.title: Graphs and Traversal

class1.name: Search in a Binary Search Tree
Second Minimum Node in a Binary Tree

objectives: DFS in a grid
Tree traversal
Tree creation, adjacency lists

weeks.title: BFS

class1.name: As Far from Land As Possible

objectives: BFS with counting steps or layers should be as easy as as a for loop.
DFS should be easy and natural for traversal and changing what you return should not be too foreign.

class1.name: Shortest Path in a Grid with Obstacles Elimination

BFS starting sets

weeks.title: DP

class2.name: Number of Dice Rolls with Target Sum

outside.notes: 4 other *new* problems of any category any difficulty

outside.name: Dynamic Programming from Cracking the Coding Interview Page 33 Chapter 8

objectives: DP as DFS with memoization
DP as not re calculating what you already calculated
Designing a DP solution to fit time and size constraints considering efficiency tradeoffs for different framings of the problem.

weeks.title: DP, Counting, Prefix

class1.name: Number of Ways to Select Buildings

outside.name: Number of Boomerangs
outside.notes: *my least favorite type of problem... I don't recommend it, but it might be good for you...*

objectives: Counting problems as a subset of DP
Prefix and postfix creating faster ways to get data
DP as a way to pass once and remember as you go what you need

weeks.title: DP

class1.name: Intersection of Two Linked Lists
class1.notes: *Could you write a solution that runs in O(m + n) time and use only O(1) memory?*

class2.name: Number of Increasing Paths in a Grid

outside.name: Interview Ready extension
outside.notes: *Go for extra credit! Get every category “green”:*

objectives: Linked lists review
DP as DFS with memoization, DFS with constraints
DP considering what unique ways means
Prepare for interviews by practicing common problems

weeks.title: SQL, DP

class1.notes: Random problem or voted on problem from the OA

outside.notes: Do problems from the OA, review, or explore something new! 4 problems

objectives: SQL awareness
DP awareness that they can be very difficult even if you've studied

weeks.title: Logarithmic Algorithms, Two Pointer, Heaps
objectives: Cut problems in half to cut runtime from N to LogN (e.g. a million steps cut down to twenty steps)
Priority queues are very useful to find a the smallest item in a frequently added to set.
Two pointer, sorting, heapifying strategies for overlapping intervals
Expose those who are bored with this class to something interesting in the Longest Duplicate Substring problem

weeks.title: Two Pointer, Faster Sort, Heap, Segment Tree

outside.name: Interview Ready extension
outside.notes: *Go for extra credit! Get every category “green”:*

objectives: Identify and apply appropriate alogrithms to a problem with unknown category
Creating and using structures that give logN complexity
Finding even better time complexities than you thought possible

problems.name: Number of Ways to Select Buildings
Number of Dice Rolls with Target Sum

objectives: Implement DP solutions using DFS with memoization (boolean, sum, min/max)
Implement counting solutions where a formula is used to increment a total count at each iteration (e.g. N(N-1)/2 for subsequences)

attempts.desc: Solve all these problems in 2 hours, no external help from friends, solutions, AI, TA, etc

problems.name: How Many Numbers Are Smaller Than the Current Number

attempts.desc: Solve 3 of these problems with at least at least 1 of the 3 solved in under 25 minutes

problems.name: Minimum Number of Arrows to Burst Balloons
Search a 2D Matrix
problems.notes: Runtime O(log(m * n)) or better, Space O(1)

problems.name: Minimum Score by Changing Two Elements

attempts.desc: Solve all these problems *no time limit* with (or without) realtime two-way interactive help from live humans

objectives: Perform an implementation of binary search in common contexts (rotated int array, median of two sorted arrays)
Apply binary search in a variety of novel contexts (e.g. space coordinates, number lists, strings, graphs, etc)
Perform an implementation of common search algorithms (quicksort, mergesort, bucket sort or counting sort)
Perform an implementation that uses an ordered structure or creates an ordered structure to solve a novel problem.
Priority Queue (Heap) problems.
Implement various two-pointer algorithms.
Perform an implementation of algorithms that require iterating through a data set and keeping track of multiple things (intervals, multiple constraints).

question.name: LRU Cache
question.desc:
Design a data structure with `O(1)` `get` and `put` that evicts the least-recently-used key
when at capacity. A strong solution combines a hash map with a doubly linked list.

objectives: Implement solutions requiring hashset, dictionaries, or key/value constant-time lookup structures.
Implement solutions involving 2D arrays.
Implement solutions involving iterating over characters of a string.
Implement solutions involving modifying a linked list.
Implement solutions requiring indexing into a linked list from a dictionary.

question.name: Course Schedule IV
question.desc:
Given prerequisite pairs, answer queries about whether one course is a prerequisite
(directly or indirectly) of another. Exercises directed-graph reachability — DFS/BFS from
each node, or a transitive-closure approach.

objectives: Implement solutions requiring DFS.
Implement solutions requiring BFS.
Implement solutions requiring cycle detection in a graph.

question.name: Number of Dice Rolls With Target Sum
question.desc:
Count the ways to roll `n` dice with `k` faces each to reach a target sum, modulo
`10^9 + 7`. A classic counting DP — DFS with memoization over `(dice remaining, target)`, or
a bottom-up table.

objectives: Implement DP solutions using DFS with memoization (boolean, sum, min/max).
Implement counting solutions where a formula increments a total count at each iteration (e.g., N(N-1)/2 for subsequences).
Implement solutions that use a prefix or postfix to be more efficient.
Implement bottom-up solutions that build from a base case iteratively.

question.name: Design Twitter
question.desc:
Design a simplified Twitter supporting `postTweet`, `follow`, `unfollow`, and a
`getNewsFeed` that returns the 10 most recent tweets from a user and everyone they follow.
Exercises merging ordered streams with a **heap / priority queue** plus hash maps for the
follow graph.

objectives: Implement binary search in common contexts (rotated int array, median of two sorted arrays).
Apply binary search in novel contexts (space coordinates, number lists, strings, graphs, etc.).
Implement common search algorithms (quicksort, mergesort, bucket/counting sort).
Use or create an ordered structure to solve a novel problem.
Solve priority queue (heap) problems.
Implement various two-pointer algorithms.
Iterate through a data set while tracking multiple things (intervals, multiple constraints).

liveInterview.graderRating: The TA or instructor also awards a 1/2/3; a 3 means they'd want to hire you.

final.partialCredit: passing half the test cases counts as a partial on up to 2 problems; two partials = one solved (up to two partials)

assignments.title: Connect with Class
assignments.desc: Join the class Microsoft Teams and update your photo.

assignments.title: Instructor Pass/Fail Interview
assignments.desc: Required to pass the course. A random-topic live interview with the instructor; be caught up on topic exams and have passed at least one performance exam first.

assignments.title: Add a Feature or Fix a Bug
